title_en: Thinking of Switching Phone Carriers? T-Mobile Will Pay Off Up to $800 of Your Remaining Phone Balance If You Do
summary_en: The entire process now takes only 15 minutes.
body_en:
Editor's Note: This article is sponsored by T-Mobile.
If you're thinking of switching providers but you're mired in phone payments that you can't get out of, then you're in luck. As part of its "Keep and Switch" program , T-Mobile, America's best mobile network according to Ookla Speedtest, will pay off up to $800 from your existing phone balance when you switch over. Even better, its AI-guided online switching service is fast and easy to use. You can now get through the entire process, one and done, in 15 minutes or less.
If you're a current Verizon, AT&T, Spectrum, Xfinity, UScellular, Claro, or Liberty subscriber, T-Mobile will pay up to $800 of your remaining phone balance when you bring over your phone and port in your number. In order to redeem the offer, you'll need to check your phone eligibility (most recent iPhone, Google Pixel, Samsung Galaxy, and Motorola models qualify), take a screenshot of your current phone balance, and submit the rebate. The credit will be delivered to you within 15 to 30 days in the form of a virtual MasterCard debit card that's valid for six months.
T-Mobile's online switching is fast and easy. The process takes about 15 minutes, not including device activation or data and number transfer. An AI guide will even help narrow down the best plans for you based on your budget and needs.
If you want to keep things simple, check out the Essentials Saver plan, which is T-Mobile's most affordable single-line unlimited plan at $50 per month with AutoPay. The plan includes unlimited talk and text, 50GB of premium data (including 5G and 4G LTE), unlimited talk/text/data in Canada and Mexico, and unlimited text in 215+ countries and destinations.
This is an especially great opportunity for anyone who is unhappy with their current service but feels tethered by existing payments. Unfortunately, many of us would rather stick with our current situation than deal with the hassle of switching. Fortunately, T-Mobile makes the process as hassle-free as possible by expediting the entire process and giving you an "out" on your existing payments. It's definitely worth checking out.
Eric Song is the IGN commerce manager in charge of finding the best gaming and tech deals every day. When Eric isn't hunting for deals for other people at work, he's hunting for deals for himself during his free time.
